---
title: "Roadmap"
description: "Milestones and the prioritized issue backlog."
---

The strategy is a **vertical slice**: build the hardest, most-differentiating piece
(a tactical battle) first, make it fun, then grow outward into a campaign.

## Milestones

::: {#tbl-milestones}

| Milestone | Scope | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **M1** | One playable, self-contained tactical battle. | ✅ Scaffolded & playable |
| **M2** | A dynastic **campaign map** --- clickable provinces, characters/realms, turn-based diplomacy & war. Battles **auto-resolved** at first. | 🚧 In progress --- the Gallic War map, data-driven campaigns, and diplomacy (with AI agency) have landed; characters/dynasty and the saga layer remain |
| **M3** | **Integration** --- an army battle on the campaign map launches into the M1 battle scene and returns a result (winner, casualties). Where the two genres meet. | 🚧 In progress --- the battle hand-off and result-applied-to-map round trip have landed; AI-vs-AI/AI-vs-player battle launches and richer army composition remain |

The three milestones and their status.
:::

The battle scene was kept self-contained specifically to make the M3 hand-off clean.

## Prioritized backlog

The foundation, the collision pillar (per-type footprint, pathfinding, formation
cohesion, hard blocking), and the shared collision-exemption primitive (friendly
pass-through, line relief, unit merging) all shipped --- see
[`PLAN.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/PLAN.md)'s design-pillars
section for how each landed. With that early backlog cleared, the issue tracker
itself is the live source of truth for what's prioritized now: browse
[open issues on `Lacaedemon/sparta`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/issues)
with `P0`--`P3` labels, or the two dominant current threads below.

Two threads dominate the current `P2` backlog, both feeding the M2/M3 milestones
above:

- **Campaign layer** --- [phase 1 through phase 7](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+%22Campaign+phase%22+in%3Atitle)
  build out map/time, army composition, battle-resolution modes, real-geography
  ingestion, logistics, sagas, and dynasty in sequence.
- **Battle AI** --- [phase 2 through phase 5](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+%22Battle+AI+phase%22+in%3Atitle)
  build subcommanders, a general layer with doctrine and reserves, player
  delegation to AI subcommanders, and fog-of-war-aware perception.

Independent of those two threads, `P2`/`P3` also carries near-term fixes and
design work (formation/spawn edge cases, the soldier weapon/shield type model,
a maneuver-queue refactor) --- the tracker's labels and open-issue list are more
current than any snapshot repeated here.

---

*Source: [`PLAN.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/PLAN.md) in the
repository. The issue tracker is the live source of truth for status.*
